name: cli
description: Cross-cutting CLI infrastructure. Provides the cobra root command, the version subcommand + --version/-v flag, and the subcommand-registration framework that dispatches every portitor command — operator subcommands and the load-bearing machine entrypoints alike — with universal --help/-h/help at the root and every subcommand. Mirrors spexmachina's cli module so the toolchain is dispatched the same way. cobra is a compile-time dependency confined to this layer; the gate/rules/check modules do not import it and their only-runtime-dependency-is-git property is unchanged. NOTE: hand-authored SEED in spexmachina's format; identity hashes (requirement id/preq_id, component spec_node_id) and spec/.snapshot.json are generated when run through spex / the /spec skill; they are intentionally omitted here.

requirements.title: A cobra command tree with universal help
requirements.description: A top-level cobra root (cli.NewRootCmd) registers every subcommand — the operator subcommands (init-repo, add-repo, upgrade-repo, reconcile, add-role, validate-config, upgrade, pr) and the machine entrypoints (pre-receive, post-receive, shell, and the hidden internal-check-exec) — mirroring spexmachina's cli/root.go + cmd/spex/*.go split (one newXxxCmd() per subcommand). --help/-h/help print usage to stdout and exit 0 uniformly at the root and at every subcommand, cobra-generated from the registered tree and flag definitions. Help is grouped (gate / provisioning / action channel) to reproduce the old usage() structure. The root has SilenceUsage/SilenceErrors set (main owns error reporting and the exit-code mapping, so a rejected push never prints command usage), and cobra's default `completion` command is disabled so the command set does not grow; the `help` command is kept.

requirements.title: Version subcommand and --version/-v flag
requirements.description: `portitor version` and the root's --version/-v flag both print `portitor <version> (commit <commit>, built <date>)` to stdout and exit 0. The values are the package-level main.version/commit/date variables injected at build time via ldflags (default dev/none/unknown for a local build); --version/-v use cobra's built-in version flag wired to the same line via SetVersionTemplate, so all three forms are byte-identical.

requirements.title: Behavior-preserving dispatch for the machine entrypoints
requirements.description: Migrating from the hand-rolled `switch os.Args[1]` to cobra must not let cobra's flag/arg validation break the non-human entrypoints. pre-receive/post-receive take no user flags and read ref updates from stdin (Args: cobra.ArbitraryArgs so cobra never rejects git's argless invocation; the RunE reads cmd.InOrStdin() and returns the verdict). shell <fingerprint> is the SSH forced command whose argv is operator-controlled; its RunE hands the args to the shell dispatcher untouched, and its pr route re-executes the same cobra pr command with the connection fingerprint injected as the actor identity. internal-check-exec is a Hidden subcommand with DisableFlagParsing (the operator-configured command it trampolines into may carry arbitrary flags cobra must not parse); its handler calls os.Exit directly so no allocation unwinds back through cobra after the rlimit is applied. Provisioning defaults and every stdin/exit contract are unchanged.

requirements.title: Preserve the process exit-code contract
requirements.description: A command that ran returns its own exit code (0/1/2) out of RunE via a typed *exitError whose Error() is empty (the command already emitted its own diagnostics); main uses only the code. Any other Execute error is a cobra usage error — unknown subcommand, unknown/malformed flag, wrong arg count — and maps to exit 2, matching the hand-rolled dispatcher's usage-error code. A rejected push exits 1, an operational error exits 1, and a command's own validation error exits 2; the gate accept/reject codes and every code callers or tests depend on are preserved.

requirements.title: Self-update subcommand
requirements.description: `portitor upgrade` self-updates the installed standalone binary by driving the embedded, already-signed install.sh — it does NOT reimplement resolve/download/verify/replace. It resolves the running binary's real path (os.Executable + filepath.EvalSymlinks), writes the embedded script to a private 0600 temp file, and runs `sh <file>` synchronously in upgrade mode against that path, streaming the script's output and propagating its exit code. Flags: --check/--dry-run (report current vs latest, change nothing), --version vX.Y.Z (pin, passed via the script's VERSION env), --rollback (restore <path>.bak), --force (allow a downgrade). It passes its compiled-in main.version as --current so the downgrade guard does not depend on re-executing the about-to-be-replaced binary. It is registered in the provisioning group, distinct from the pre-existing upgrade-repo command, and its help states it maintains the standalone binary only — the container image is a separate Dockerfile artifact it does not touch. The mechanism it drives is specified by the delivery module's SelfUpdate component; the embedded script is byte-identical to the canonical repo-root install.sh (a go test enforces the identity).

requirements.title: cobra is a compile-time dependency only
requirements.description: github.com/spf13/cobra (version matching spexmachina) is added to go.mod, pulling in github.com/spf13/pflag and github.com/inconshreveable/mousetrap as indirects. cobra is a build-time library baked into the binary; it does not change portitor's only-runtime-dependency-is-git property — the running binary still needs only git on the host. cobra is confined to the CLI layer (cli/, cmd/portitor/); the gate, rules, and check modules do not import it.

components.name: RootCommand
components.description: cli.NewRootCmd: the top-level `portitor` cobra command. Sets Use/Short/Long, SilenceUsage/SilenceErrors, disables the default completion command, and registers the gate/provisioning/action command groups. Not runnable on its own; cmd/portitor's newRootCommand() sets the version metadata (--version/-v) and adds every subcommand.

components.name: VersionCommand
components.description: `portitor version` — prints `portitor <version> (commit <commit>, built <date>)` via printVersion(cmd.OutOrStdout()); the same line backs the root's --version/-v flag. Values injected via ldflags at build time.

components.name: UpgradeCommand
components.description: `portitor upgrade` — the thin Go front-end (newUpgradeCmd + upgradeRun in cmd/portitor) that self-updates the installed standalone binary by embedding the canonical install.sh and running it in upgrade mode against the running binary's resolved path. Flags --check/--dry-run, --version, --rollback, --force; reimplements no update logic (that lives in install.sh — see the delivery module's SelfUpdate). Distinct from upgrade-repo.

components.name: MachineEntrypointDispatch
components.description: The cobra wrapping of the load-bearing entrypoints: pre-receive/post-receive (ArbitraryArgs, stdin, verdict-as-exit-code), shell <fingerprint> (SSH forced command; pr route re-executes the cobra pr command with the connection fingerprint injected), and the hidden internal-check-exec trampoline (DisableFlagParsing, os.Exit). Plus the *exitError plumbing and the usage-error → exit 2 mapping that preserve the process exit-code contract.

data_flows.name: Command Dispatch and Exit Codes
data_flows.description: How user (and machine) input flows from argv through cobra to the correct subcommand handler, and how each handler's result becomes the process exit code.
